# Git commands to deploy Jekyll site to GitHub Pages
# Run these from your local machine

# 1. Navigate to the deploy folder
cd /home/billy/.openclaw/workspace/billys-system/projects/billyjhart-site/dev/developer/jekyll-site/deploy-2026-02-28-141400/

# 2. Initialize git repository
git init

# 3. Add GitHub remote (replace YOURUSERNAME with your GitHub username)
git remote add origin https://github.com/billyjhart/billyjhart.github.io.git

# 4. Add all Jekyll files
git add .

# 5. Commit with deployment message
git commit -m "Deploy Jekyll site - converted from static HTML"

# 6. Push and replace existing files (force push to replace static site)
git branch -M main
git push -f origin main

# Note: The -f (force) flag replaces all existing files in the repository
# This is safe since we're intentionally replacing the static site with Jekyll
